summary: Updated the EditorConfig GitHub Actions workflow to be more portable and more informative: pinned Ubuntu to 22.04, removed hardcoded doldecomp repository references, expanded excluded generated/tool paths, checked out the PR head ref for pull_request_target, ran editorconfig-checker verbosely, and wrote the checked file list to the GitHub step summary.
what_changed: Renamed the workflow and check step from "Checking EditorConfig" to "Check EditorConfig".
Changed the runner from ubuntu-latest to ubuntu-22.04 for a fixed Ubuntu version.
Removed the job condition that restricted execution to github.repository_owner == 'doldecomp', leaving only the '[skip editorconfig]' PR-title skip condition.
Changed the GitHub API path for changed files from repos/doldecomp/melee/... to repos/${{github.repository}}/..., allowing the workflow to run on PRs against any repository copy.
Expanded the grep exclude filter from only tools/m2c to generated or non-source paths: dump, build, expected, tools/m2c, and tools/calcprogress.
Changed checkout ref under pull_request_target from refs/pull/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/merge to ${{github.head_ref}}.
Added verbose mode to editorconfig-checker via -v while retaining -disable-indent-size.
Added a GitHub step summary listing the changed files checked by EditorConfig.
smart_moves: Using github.repository instead of a hardcoded owner/repo made the workflow reusable outside doldecomp/melee and fixed fork or copied-repository usability.
Pinning ubuntu-22.04 avoided potential breakage from ubuntu-latest image changes.
The broader exclude regex prevented EditorConfig checks from wasting time or failing on generated/build/expected output and specific tool directories.
Verbose editorconfig-checker output and a GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY file list made CI failures easier for contributors to diagnose.
The workflow preserved the existing '[skip editorconfig]' escape hatch while removing the repository-owner gate.
decomp_lessons: CI that validates formatting should avoid generated decomp artifacts such as dump, build, and expected directories, because those paths are not the source style signal reviewers care about.
Repository-specific strings in GitHub Actions make decomp workflows harder to reuse in forks and related repositories; prefer GitHub context variables such as github.repository.
For pull_request_target workflows, be explicit about which ref is checked out because the event defaults can differ from ordinary pull_request behavior.
When style tooling fails, contributor-facing diagnostics matter: verbose tool output and a step summary reduce back-and-forth during review.
naming_conventions: Prefer concise imperative GitHub Actions names such as "Check EditorConfig" over gerund phrasing like "Checking EditorConfig".
Use the PR title marker '[skip editorconfig]' as the workflow skip convention.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided slice.
One review is recorded, but no review body or actionable reviewer feedback is included in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modified .github/workflows/editorconfig.yml to see whether github.head_ref or the pull_request_target checkout behavior needed further fixes.
Search for uses of '[skip editorconfig]' to confirm the skip-title convention remained accepted.
Look for CI failures involving editorconfig-checker after PR 748 to validate whether the expanded exclude filter covered all generated paths.
